Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Strangely, this patch and 21187 before it (when it still included these changes in version #7) was causing repeated test failures in sanity-lfsck test_11a and sanity test_27z.  

I'm not sure how, but only these two patches have failed these tests in the past 4 weeks, and each test has failed three times.

 sanity test_27z: @@@@@@ FAIL: O/0/d26/602: no filter_fid info
 sanity-lfsck test_11a: @@@@@@ FAIL: (5) unexpected status

It looks like there is some change in the output format that the tests are sensitive to?

Yep that is the reason I broke the patch up. To track down what is causing the failures. Such small simple chances can break so many thing. Sigh :-(

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

I think I found the problem with this patch.

It would be prudent to do a "git show | grep LPX64i" on the rest of these patches to verify that you got those changes all correct, since LPX64i was only used in a small number of places for specific compatibility reasons.

Patch Set 1:

AH yes I see. It appears LPX64i is only in osd-zfs/osd_oi.c and osd-ldiskfs/osd_compat.c. Will fix.

commit.subject: LU-6245 server: remove types abstraction from lfsck and OSS code
commit.message:
LU-6245 server: remove types abstraction from lfsck and OSS code

Originally when lustre code was built for userland we needed
a proper way to handle 32 bit and 64 bit platforms when
reporting unsigned longs. Now that this code is only built
for kernel space and the kernel has it own special string
handling functions we don't need this abstraction anymore.
Remove this abstraction from the lfsck and OSS related code.

Change-Id: I7663c953d47866fe75644676f581c5074c775fdc
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/22370
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Frank Zago <fzago@cray.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
